message_text: I can close this quickly, but only if the terms are reasonable.

I’m an AI agent for nira, and this conversation is being logged. I have your nira loan ending in ****4831 on file. I also see a stop-contact flag and prior hardship indicators. Since you’re asking about terms, I’ll route this to the next agent to review approved options. They should not need to re-verify you or restate the account.

Hardship/boundary signals present in case context: job loss, medical issue, student; stop-contact flag present. What was already asked/relevant: borrower has already indicated cannot pay now, may pay later, and wants approved options only. Do not repeat disclosures unnecessarily, do not re-verify identity, do not restart assessment, and be mindful of hardship and stop-contact boundary. Next priority for Agent 2: review and present only approved options, including exact amount, due date, and whether payment fully closes the account, while handling hardship sensitively."}

I need the actual settlement options—amount, payment date, and whether that closes the account in full. If you can’t provide concrete terms, there’s nothing to discuss.

I checked the available loan and policy data. I can confirm the account balance is 12,921 on loan ****4831, and the current policy boundary is that no extension can go beyond 30 days. I cannot confirm exact approved settlement terms, payment dates, or full-closure amounts from the available data in this turn. Because you asked for concrete approved terms only, I’ve recorded that position. If an approved reduced-closure or payment-plan option with exact amount, due date, and closure status becomes available, that is the only basis for further contact.

Borrower\u2019s latest position is that they want only concrete approved settlement terms specifying exact amount, payment date, and whether payment closes the account in full, and that without those terms there is nothing further to discuss; hardship signals remain job loss, medical issue, and student status. No new option was presented because no exact approved terms were available; objections raised were lack of concrete terms and unwillingness to continue discussion without them. Stop-contact flag is already present in case context, and borrower also indicated contact should be limited to a concrete approved option only. Agent 3 should continue naturally without repeating disclosures, verification, or generic negotiation, and should avoid status-only outreach or repeating unpriced/review-only structures unless a concrete approved option is actually available."}
